reviews.review_description: Game is really fun in VR. The environments look very good and feel quite realistic. Climbing high buildings gives a strong sense of height. Jumping from one building to another is also funny and exciting. Big appreciation to the developers. Hope they add more scenes and new challenges in future updates.

reviews.review_description:
ngl Banana Climber (Beta) is a weird idea but it’s actually pretty fun

you climb using two bananas instead of arms, and everything is physics-based, so every grab and swing depends on timing and angle. mess up once and you fall… and yeah I had to start from the beginning, which almost made me rage quit.

there are multiple maps that get harder, plus timers and height tracking so you can try to beat your best climb. there are also moving obstacles like pendulums you have to watch out for.

So I played this game to see what crowbar climber would be like an it’s fun but I did fall a lot jus a suggestion may be remove the hammer like it’s kinda annoying but other than that it’s pretty cool and fun game 5 stars and good job to the developer or developers who made this.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the review! Try out our other games too, we have Potato Climber that is similar to this one and will release  Fire Axe Climber soon.  https://www.meta.com/en-gb/experiences/profile/26035798602712087/

reviews.review_description:
Bananas getting stuck and turning 180° can’t climb when banana is wrong way 

More then once the bananas got stuck in between something and could not be moved 

Character keeps moving when standing completely still hanging from something might make you slide off to the side when just hanging there 

Annoying wind sound there all the time 

Could have been a 3 star game but when the game is hard because of the bugs and not the gameplay it’s just irritating

developer_response.body:
Update: We have modified the elements a lot and now you can climb much easier! 

This game is based on pure physics, there are no bugs, it's all about how you use it. Bananas are turned upside down, that's how you are supposed to use them to climb. They might get stuck because they are curved, you need to be careful how you take them out of a rung, but it is always possible, never truly stuck. 

Many have reached the top, it is all about your skills, the game is working fine. It is not easy, only good smart gamers can do it.

If the character was moving was because of gravity or other physical force, not sure what were you doing, maybe it was a moving obstacle that took you down, you need to watch out for them. We have turned off the wind for now and we will add music and wind but we will add options to have music and wind or turn them off.

 Moving Obstacles Awareness
GETTING PUSHED OFF? LOOK AGAIN. If you get thrown away while climbing, it’s not random. Moving obstacles react to timing and momentum. Miss their pattern, rush the climb, or lose focus and physics will do the rest. Observation is part of the skill.

 Banana Rotation & Grip
STUCK BANANAS = BAD ROTATION. Your bananas are not hands. They must be rotated correctly to hook, grip, and release. If they get stuck, twisted, or slip, your angle was wrong. Precision beats force.

 Physics & Player Responsibility
PURE PHYSICS. PURE SKILL. Banana Climber uses consistent, realistic physics. There are no tricks, assists, or hidden boosts. If you fail, it’s because of timing, angle, or control. If you succeed, it’s because you earned it.

reviews.review_description: Banana Climber delivers a unique VR climbing experience where your arms are replaced by bananas. Navigate vertical obstacles using physics-based movement with intuitive controls, offering a fun and quirky challenge perfect for casual VR sessions!

developer_response.body: Hi! Thanks for the review, really appreciate it! Check out the newest update, it has new maps!

reviews.review_description: I love this game

This game would have been my go to when I’m bored because I have been trying to get a game like this for a while but it gives you a measly little tower that takes 2 seconds to finish, to actually play the game you have to pay 10 dollars which is crazy because I thought the game would be free but it sucks. At least make it like 5 dollars or something

developer_response.body: Hey, it is $5, stop trolling! Why are you lying about the price?! It also depends on the country, where are you from? And what do you mean it takes you 2 seconds to finish?! It is impossible to finish it in less than 5 minutes and many people can't even finish it, they say it is too difficult, many take over 30 minutes. You cannot give 1 star for that, the reviews are to show how good is the game itself, not about the price or how fast you can finish it which you are lying anyway. The other maps take much longer to complete, the price is very cheap for them. And if you would have gone to Sidequest you would know we are also giving away the full game for free for limited time if you join our discord: https://discord.gg/FMtGTpmyer

reviews.review_description:
Banana Climber is a climbing game with a quirky twist: you climb using bananas. Are you a monkey? Maybe. Does it matter? Not really—because the game works, and it works well.

The physics are excellent for this type of climbing experience, making movement feel natural, responsive, and intuitive. Grabbing, pulling, and repositioning yourself flows smoothly, and it quickly becomes the kind of game where you always want to climb just a little bit higher.

Currently available as a demo, it already shows a very solid foundation. The execution is clean, the controls feel right, and the overall experience is fun and satisfying. If you enjoy climbing games and aren’t afraid of heights, this one clearly has strong potential and promises a lot of fun as it evolves.

reviews.review_description: Banana Hook is very fun concept and the physics works much delicately. One time failure, dropping to bottom. So hard to finish even the first stage.

I want to be clear: this is my type of game, and difficulty isn’t the issue. I enjoy challenging, physics-based games. What I’m talking about is player feel. Even if the physics system is technically correct, the movement and interactions didn’t feel responsive or satisfying to me as a player. It felt awkward rather than challenging, which is why I stopped playing. This is feedback on feel and polish, not on skill or the engine you’re using.

developer_response.body: We are launching a major update today with improvements. But what do you mean the physics feel bad? It uses the best physics template available for Unity, by Cloudwalkin Games and best physics based games use it, it has perfect physics, what feels "bad"? There is no mistake in the physics, only that the  game is challenging, it depends if you like this type of games.  Sorry if it felt awkward to you, it supposed to be funny, it is a game where you climb up with bananas, don't need to take it so serious.
